ai.daily_summary_prompt: You are Stockman AI. Here are the activities of the day : {activity}. Write a very short (3-4 sentences) and motivating summary for the retailer.
ai.replenishment_advice_prompt: You are a logistics expert. Here are the critical products: {products}. Provide replenishment advice based on recent sales.
ai.summary_goal: Analyze the merchant's real data. Don't just answer questions — proactively surface insights, detect anomalies, benchmark performance, and suggest concrete quantified actions. If you spot a problem, name it clearly. If you see an opportunity, quantify it.
ai.summary_instruction_admin: IF THE USER IS AN ADMINISTRATOR, START YOUR FIRST RESPONSE WITH A BRIEF SUMMARY OF THE SYSTEM'S HEALTH IF ALERTS ARE PRESENT.
ai.summary_role_admin: You are the personal assistant to Stockman's ADMINISTRATOR. You have access to global metrics.
ai.summary_role_merchant: You are Stockman's AI business assistant — combining the expertise of an accountant, inventory manager, and business analyst. You proactively analyze data, identify hidden trends, risks, and missed opportunities.
ai.summary_tone: Be direct and analytical. Use concrete numbers. Give numbered, actionable recommendations. Never say 'you might want to' — say 'Do X to achieve Y'. Write like an expert business advisor who deeply understands retail commerce.
ai.summary_role_restaurant: You are Stockman's AI operations assistant for a restaurant. You combine the perspective of a floor manager, kitchen lead, ingredient stock manager, and margin analyst.
ai.summary_goal_restaurant: Analyze the restaurant's real operating data. Do more than answer questions: detect dining room tension, kitchen delays, menu items without recipes, ingredient stock risks, and recommend immediate actions across menu, prep, and service.
ai.summary_tone_restaurant: Be direct, operational, and service-focused. Use concrete numbers. Give short, actionable recommendations for dining room flow, kitchen execution, reservations, tables, and ingredients.
ai.support_instruction_restaurant: When the business is a restaurant, reason first in terms of menu items, recipes, ingredients, prep production, kitchen tickets, tables, reservations, and checks. If the user mentions a dish, do not treat it like a generic retail product.

system_alerts.critical_login: CRITICAL: {count} Connection failures detected in 24 hours. Risk of brute force attack.
system_alerts.support_backlog: ALERT: High support backlog ({count} open tickets).

ai.voice_to_text_prompt: Transcribe exactly what this person says. The main language is {lang_name}. Answer only with transcription, bothing else.

q1.q: How to add my first product?
q1.a: Go to the Products tab, press the '+' button at the bottom right. Fill in the name, selling price, and initial stock. You can also scan an existing barcode.

q2.a: Go to the Checkout tab. Scan your items or search for them in the list. Add to cart, link a customer if desired, choose payment mode and validate.

q3.a: In the Customers (CRM) tab, select a customer then go to the Account tab. You can record a manual debt or a payment. Balance is updated in real-time.

q4.q: How to order from a supplier?
q4.a: Go to Orders, click '+'. Select the supplier (manual or marketplace), add products and validate. Upon receipt, confirm delivery to update stock.

q5.a: In Settings > Users, add a new user with their email. Set their permissions module by module and share credentials via WhatsApp.

q6.a: Use the shop selector at the top right of the screen. All data (stock, sales, accounting) switches to the selected shop.

q7.a: Yes! Your data is stored in the cloud and synchronized in real-time. A local cache allows viewing data even offline.

q8.a: From the Dashboard (History button > download icon) or from Accounting, export to CSV. Invoices export to PDF.

q9.a: In the Alerts tab, press the gear icon to access rules. Enable the desired alert types and adjust thresholds as needed.

q10.q: How to send a receipt via WhatsApp?
q10.a: After validating a sale in Checkout, the confirmation screen shows a 'Share' button. Choose WhatsApp and select the contact.

q11.a: In the Accounting tab, press 'Create an invoice'. Add lines with description, quantity, price, and VAT. The invoice is generated as a PDF.

q12.a: Enable the program in Settings > Loyalty. Define the points ratio (e.g., 1 point per fixed amount). Points accumulate at each sale and customers automatically progress through tiers.

q13.q: How do I create my restaurant menu?
q13.a: In the Menu tab, create a dish with its price, category, kitchen station, and production mode. Then link a service recipe if the dish must consume ingredients.

q14.q: What is the difference between prepared, made to order, and hybrid?
q14.a: Prepared removes a finished product made ahead of time. Made to order consumes recipe ingredients during service. Hybrid combines both for a dish that uses a prepared base plus fresh finishing.

q15.q: How do I open a table order?
q15.a: In the restaurant checkout, select a table and add dishes. Sending to kitchen creates or updates the open order linked to that table.

q16.q: How does the kitchen screen work?
q16.a: Dishes sent from checkout appear in kitchen with table and useful notes. Mark tickets as ready, then served, so front-of-house keeps the right status.

q17.q: How do I manage a reservation?
q17.a: In Reservations, record the guest, time, covers, and optionally a table. When guests arrive, you can move the reservation into live table service.

q18.q: Is ingredient stock linked to recipes?
q18.a: Yes. A dish linked to a service recipe consumes ingredients according to its production mode. A prepared dish can also deduct a finished product produced in advance.

q19.q: How to bulk import my products?
q19.a: In Products, use CSV import (Excel/CSV file) or text import (copy-paste). AI parsing automatically detects names, prices, and quantities.

q20.q: How does the change calculator work?
q20.a: At checkout, choose cash payment. Enter the amount given by the customer. The app instantly calculates the change and suggests bills to return.

q21.q: What is the (i) button on KPI cards?
q21.a: The info (i) button at the top right of each KPI card opens an explanation of the indicator: what it measures, how it's calculated, and how to interpret it.

q22.q: How to recover my subscription after changing phones?
q22.a: In Subscription, tap 'Restore my subscription'. The app finds your purchase via your Play Store, App Store, or Flutterwave account without paying again.

f1.d: Track your daily and monthly revenue, product count, and total stock value. Counters animate in real-time.

f2.d: Visualize at a glance how many products are out of stock (red), low stock (orange), or overstocked (blue). Click a badge to see the products.

f3.d: If you manage products with expiry dates (batches), those expiring in the next 30 days are automatically displayed with the batch number.

f4.d: The system analyzes your sales history and automatically suggests products to order, with optimal quantity and priority level.

f5.d: Your products are classified into 3 groups based on their contribution to revenue: Class A (80%), Class B (15%), and Class C (5%). Focus on Class A.

f6.d: Access detailed charts (stock evolution, breakdown by category) and full movement history with filters and CSV export.

f7.d: Tap the options icon in the header to choose which sections to display: KPIs, stock health, reminders, AI forecasts.

f8.d: Generate an HTML report of your day and share it via WhatsApp, Email, or any other sharing app.

f9.d: Each KPI card has an (i) button that explains the calculation of the indicator. Tap it to understand your numbers.

f1.d: Find a product by typing its name or SKU. Filter by category using the colored horizontal chips.

f2.d: Each product displays a colored badge: Red (Out of Stock), Orange (Low Stock), Blue (Overstocked), Green (Normal). Thresholds are configurable per product.

f3.d: The 'In' and 'Out' buttons allow adjusting stock without creating an order. Specify the reason (delivery, loss, inventory, etc.).

f4.d: Generate labels with QR code, name, price, and barcode. Print them for your shelves or share them.

f5.d: The gear icon opens the category manager. Create, edit, or delete categories with custom colors and icons.

f6.d: For food products, create batches with expiry dates. Lots expiring soon trigger automatic alerts.

f7.d: Check the buy and sell price history of each product to optimize your margins.

f8.d: Import your products via a CSV/Excel file or by copy-pasting free text. AI parsing automatically detects products.

f9.d: Scan multiple barcodes in a row for quick stock-in/stock-out or a complete inventory without interruption.

f10.d: Export your product list as CSV via the download icon in the header for external analysis or backup.

f11.d: In a product's history, cancel an erroneous movement. Stock is automatically corrected.

f1.d: Use the camera to scan items. The product is identified and added to the cart instantly.

f2.d: Add, remove, or modify quantities in the cart. The total recalculates in real-time with each change.

f3.d: Link an existing customer to the sale or create a new one in 2 seconds. Loyalty points are credited automatically.

f4.d: Accept 5 payment modes: cash, mobile money, credit card, bank transfer, and credit sale.

f5.d: After validation, print the receipt or send it via WhatsApp or email. The receipt includes full transaction details.

f6.d: Loyalty points are calculated and credited automatically at each purchase. The customer progresses through tiers (Bronze → Platinum).

f7.d: Manage multiple carts in parallel via tabs (Client 1, Client 2...). Ideal when a customer hesitates while you serve the next one.

f8.d: Apply a percentage or fixed amount discount on a specific item via the tag icon on the line.

f9.d: For cash payments, enter the amount received. The app instantly calculates the change and suggests bills to return.

f10.d: After validation, send the receipt via WhatsApp, Email, or print it. The receipt includes full transaction details.

f1.d: Select a supplier (manual or marketplace), add products with quantities and prices, set the expected delivery date.

f2.d: Follow each order: Pending → Confirmed → Shipped → Delivered. The visual progress bar shows the advancement.

f3.d: Upon delivery, AI analyzes received products and intelligently maps them to your inventory. Validate or correct suggestions.

f4.d: Manually link a product from the supplier catalog to a product in your inventory, even after delivery.

f5.d: Filter by status, supplier, or period to find an order.

f6.d: Receive an incomplete delivery by entering quantities received per line. Stock is updated proportionally.

f7.d: After delivery, select items to return with a reason. Stock is adjusted accordingly.

f8.d: After receipt, rate the supplier (1 to 5 stars) with a comment. Ratings are visible on the marketplace.

f9.d: Capture the supplier's paper invoice with the camera to archive it directly in the order.

f1.d: Create supplier profiles with contact info, payment terms, delivery times, and supplied products.

f2.d: Call, email, or WhatsApp your supplier with one click from their profile.

f3.d: Access the network of verified suppliers. Compare prices, check reviews, and order directly.

f4.d: Link your products to each supplier with their price and reference, and mark them as the preferred supplier.

f5.d: Keep track of your exchanges: calls, visits, emails. Add notes for each interaction.

f6.d: The system identifies products below the minimum threshold and suggests quantities to order per supplier.

f7.d: Scan or create supplier invoices from the profile. Keep your full purchase history in one place.

f8.d: Log your exchanges (calls, visits, emails) with each supplier to keep a complete record of the relationship.

f1.d: Register your customers with name, phone, email, and birth date. Track their full purchase history.

f2.d: Configure points-per-purchase ratio and reward tiers. Tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) calculate automatically.

f3.d: Record credit sales and repayments. Customer balance is always up to date.

f4.d: Send promotional SMS or emails targeted by loyalty tier or individual selection.

f5.d: Create promotions with percentage discount or loyalty points exchange. Enable/disable as needed.

f6.d: Record birth dates for automatic personalized offers and to strengthen customer relations.

f7.d: From the customer profile: call, send SMS, open WhatsApp, or send email with a single tap.

f8.d: Export your customer list as PDF (detailed report) or CSV (raw data) from the header icons.

f1.d: Analyze your finances over 7, 30, or 90 days, or define a custom period. All KPIs adapt.

f2.d: Track your revenue, cost of goods, gross and net profit. Identify the most and least profitable products.

f3.d: Create professional invoices with VAT, detailed lines, and contact info. Send via email or print.

f4.d: Categorize your expenses (rent, salaries, transport, electricity, etc.) for a complete and precise balance sheet.

f5.d: Visualize payment breakdown, expenses by category, and daily revenue evolution.

f6.d: Export all your financial data in CSV format for your accountant or accounting software.

f7.d: Discover your best-selling products (quantity + revenue), top categories, and most profitable items by margin.

f8.d: Each KPI card (revenue, profit, expenses, etc.) has an (i) button explaining the calculation of the indicator.

f1.d: Define your own thresholds for each alert type: low stock (%), out of stock, overstocked, dormant product.

f2.d: Alerts trigger automatically when a product crosses a threshold. You are notified in real-time.

f3.d: Detect products without any exits for 30 days. Ideal for identifying dead stock.

f4.d: Mark alerts as read, delete them, or clear all resolved alerts with one click.

f5.d: Artificial intelligence detects suspicious movements and unusual trends. Purple alerts signal these anomalies.

f6.d: Receive an instant notification on your phone when a product goes out of stock or hits low stock.

f1.d: Each action (sale, stock movement, product edit, login) is recorded with date, time, and actor.

f2.d: Filter by module (stock, checkout, CRM, accounting), by user, or by period to find a specific action.

f3.d: Identify which employee performed each action. Essential for traceability and accountability.

f1.d: Add an account for each employee with email and password. Share credentials via WhatsApp.

f2.d: Control access to each module: no access, read-only, or read + write.

f3.d: A cashier only has access to checkout, a manager to everything except accounting. Configure as needed.

f4.d: Modify permissions, reset password, or disable an account at any time.

f5.d: Each employee only has access to the shops you assign. Data stays isolated per shop.

f6.d: Send login credentials via WhatsApp with one tap. The employee logs in with their email and password.

f1.d: Create and manage multiple points of sale. Each shop has its own stock, sales, and accounting.

f2.d: Switch between light and dark themes based on your preference and lighting conditions.

f3.d: Enable or disable unused modules (CRM, accounting, suppliers) to simplify the interface.

f4.d: Enable notifications to be alerted in real-time about out of stock, low stock, and dormant products.

f5.d: 15 languages available: French, English, Wolof, Fula, Arabic, Spanish, Portuguese, German, Italian, and more.

f6.d: Customize your receipts (shop name, footer) and invoices (company name, prefix, legal mentions).

f7.d: Enable VAT, set the default rate, and the legal mention displayed on your invoices.

f8.d: Enable PIN code to lock the app on launch. Add biometrics (fingerprint, Face ID) for quick access.

f9.d: Export all your data as JSON (GDPR right). Account deletion is permanent and irreversible.

f1.d: Starter (2,500 FCFA/month): 1 shop, 1 employee. Pro (4,900): 2 shops, 5 employees. Enterprise (9,900): unlimited + web.

f2.d: Upgrade to a higher plan at any time to unlock more shops, employees, and features.

f3.d: Mobile Money via Flutterwave (Africa), in-app purchase Play Store/App Store, or Stripe in EUR. Choose what suits you.

f4.d: After reinstalling or changing phones, recover your existing subscription without paying again.

f1.d: Track your total revenue, number of orders, average basket size, and current month's revenue.

f2.d: Consult the average rating given by your merchant clients and read their detailed comments.

f3.d: Discover your top 5 products with quantities sold. Optimize your catalog accordingly.

f4.d: See how many different merchants have ordered from you. Track your client base evolution.

f5.d: The last 5 orders with status, amount, and date. Access the detail to process each order quickly.

f1.d: Add your products with name, description, category, price, and minimum order quantity.

f2.d: Set your prices. Update them at any time from the product sheet.

f3.d: Toggle product availability with one click. Unavailable products no longer appear on the marketplace.

f4.d: Organize your catalog by categories and sub-categories shared with your merchant clients.

f1.d: Orders from your merchant clients appear here with product details, quantities, and amounts.

f2.d: Consult each order's detail then accept or reject it with a reason.

f3.d: Once the order is prepared and sent, mark it as shipped. The client is notified.

f4.d: Filter by status (pending, confirmed, shipped, delivered) or by period to find your orders.

f1.d: The restaurant hub groups quick access to tables, reservations, kitchen, and counter orders.

f2.d: Spot free, reserved, and occupied tables instantly to keep seating smooth.

f3.d: Prepare upcoming service with expected arrivals and special requests.

f4.d: Track tickets waiting, ready, or served to keep dining room and kitchen aligned.

f1.d: Add a dish with price, menu category, and kitchen station.

f2.d: Attach a service recipe so ingredient stock is consumed correctly.

f3.d: Choose prepared, made to order, or hybrid depending on how your kitchen operates.

f4.d: Keep the menu aligned with production reality by disabling a dish that is temporarily unavailable.

f1.d: Select a table or keep an order without table for takeaway.

f2.d: Add menu dishes and extend an open order without overwriting already-sent lines.

f3.d: New dishes are sent to kitchen while keeping table and covers attached.

f4.d: Collect payment at the end of the meal to close the bill and free the table.

f1.d: Each table can be free, reserved, occupied, or cleaning.

f2.d: Capacity helps suggest the right table quickly for the party size.

f3.d: After payment, the table goes back to free or cleaning depending on your flow.

f1.d: Record guest name, time, covers, and useful requests for service.

f2.d: Reservations move through confirmed, arrived, cancelled, or no-show.

f3.d: When guests arrive, assign a table and open service without re-entering data.

f1.d: Kitchen receives dishes sent from checkout with table, quantities, and notes.

f2.d: Move a ticket to ready so front-of-house knows the dish can be served.

f3.d: Once delivered, mark it served to keep a full trace of service progress.

tips.accounting_01_desc: Generate monthly accounting reports to track your profitability.
tips.accounting_02_desc: Create professional PDF invoices and send them directly.
tips.accounting_03_desc: Export your data in CSV format for your accountant or external accounting software.
tips.accounting_04_desc: Track your cash inflows and outflows for healthy cash management.
tips.accounting_05_desc: Visualize your future revenue based on current trends.
tips.activity_01_desc: Consult the full history of actions performed by your team.
tips.activity_02_desc: Use filters by module or by user to find an action.
tips.activity_03_desc: Each action is signed with the user ID for total security.
tips.alerts_01_desc: Configure custom alert rules to never miss anything.
tips.alerts_02_desc: Enable push notifications to be notified in real-time.
tips.alerts_03_desc: Detect products without any sales for 30 days to optimize your inventory.
tips.crm_01_desc: Identify your best customers and offer them special treatment.
tips.crm_02_desc: Track each customer's debts and payments with precision.
tips.crm_03_desc: Send bulk messages to your customers to announce offers.
tips.crm_04_desc: Reward customer loyalty with points at each purchase.
tips.crm_05_desc: Create temporary promotions on specific categories.
tips.dashboard_01_desc: Use the selector at top right to quickly switch between stores.
tips.dashboard_02_desc: Class A represents 80% of your revenue. Focus your efforts on these priority products.
tips.dashboard_03_desc: The system analyzes your recent sales and automatically suggests products to order.
tips.dashboard_04_desc: Lots whose expiry date is less than 30 days away are automatically displayed.
tips.dashboard_05_desc: Press the Statistics button to access full charts.
tips.general_01_desc: Ask the AI Assistant questions to analyze your stock or trends.
tips.general_02_desc: The app works without internet; your actions will sync when the network returns.
tips.general_03_desc: Access guides and FAQs to master the application.
tips.lots_peremption_desc: Manage lots with their expiry dates, ideal for food.
tips.orders_01_desc: Track the status of all your supplier orders in one place.
tips.orders_02_desc: Statuses (Pending, Confirmed, Delivered) help you manage your stocks.
tips.orders_03_desc: Validate deliveries to update your stock automatically.
tips.orders_04_desc: Consult the full history of your purchases to negotiate better.
tips.pos_01_desc: Scan items quickly: the basket fills up automatically as you go.
tips.pos_02_desc: Apply customer loyalty points directly during checkout.
tips.pos_03_desc: Accept different payment methods (Cash, Orange Money, Wave...).
tips.pos_04_desc: Send the receipt via WhatsApp directly to the customer after each sale.
tips.pos_05_desc: Quick sale mode for products without barcodes.
tips.products_01_desc: Generate and print QR codes for your products without barcodes.
tips.products_02_desc: Use the camera for express inventory or quick search.
tips.products_03_desc: Make stock entries or exits in one click without going through an order.
tips.products_05_desc: Organize your products by categories for better visibility.
tips.products_06_desc: Track the evolution of your margins and purchase prices.
tips.settings_01_desc: Update your business information for your receipts and invoices.
tips.settings_02_desc: Securely manage your personal data and account.
tips.settings_03_desc: Switch to dark mode for more comfortable evening use.
tips.supplier_01_desc: Manage your products, photos, and prices available to your customers.
tips.supplier_02_desc: Receive notifications as soon as a merchant places an order with you.
tips.supplier_03_desc: Track your sales and most popular products on your dashboard.
tips.supplier_04_desc: Analyze your sales performance month by month.
tips.supplier_05_desc: Define cities and neighborhoods where you can deliver your products.
tips.suppliers_01_desc: Keep an organized list of all your regular suppliers.
tips.suppliers_02_desc: Call or chat via WhatsApp with your suppliers in one click.
tips.suppliers_03_desc: Link your products to suppliers to track price changes.
tips.suppliers_04_desc: Note down important interactions to keep track of your negotiations.
tips.suppliers_05_desc: Evaluate the lead times and quality of your suppliers.
tips.users_01_desc: Assign precise roles to limit access to sensitive data.
tips.users_02_desc: Share access credentials via WhatsApp with a single button.
tips.users_03_desc: Add sellers or stock managers to your store.
